title: Social Interaction: Study and Free Time

topics.name: Study
questions: What subject do you enjoy studying most?
How do you usually prepare for an exam?
Do you prefer studying alone or with friends? Why?

topics.name: Free time
questions: What do you usually do after class?
How often do you try a new hobby?
Why is free time important for students?

selection_reason: Part 1 trains familiar personal questions across two everyday topics with six short responses.

title: Social Interaction: Family and Home

topics.name: Family
questions: Who do you spend the most time with in your family?
What do family members usually do together at weekends?
Has family life changed in recent years? How?

topics.name: Home
questions: Describe the place where you live.
What is your favourite room at home?
Would you like to live in a different place in the future? Why?

selection_reason: Family and home are core social-interaction domains for B1 speaking fluency.

title: Social Interaction: Food and Health

topics.name: Food
questions: What food do you like eating on a busy day?
Do you prefer cooking at home or eating out?
What traditional food would you recommend to a visitor?

topics.name: Health
questions: How do you keep healthy?
Do you think young people exercise enough?
What habit would you like to improve?

selection_reason: Food and health support personal answers plus simple opinions in a common VSTEP domain.

title: Social Interaction: Travel and City Life

topics.name: Travel
questions: Do you enjoy travelling? Why or why not?
What place in Viet Nam would you like to visit?
What do you usually prepare before a trip?

topics.name: City life
questions: What do you like about your city or town?
How do people usually travel around your area?
Would you prefer to live in a big city or a small town? Why?

selection_reason: Travel and city-life questions prepare learners for practical description and preference language.

title: Social Interaction: Technology and Media

topics.name: Technology
questions: What device do you use most often?
How has technology changed the way you study or work?
Could people rely too much on technology? Why?

topics.name: Media
questions: Where do you usually get news?
Do you trust information on social media?
How can people check whether online information is reliable?

selection_reason: Technology/media questions elicit B2 opinion and justification while staying within Part 1 format.

title: Social Interaction: Work and Future Plans

topics.name: Work
questions: What kind of job would you like to have?
What skills are important in the workplace today?
Would you prefer working alone or in a team? Why?

topics.name: Future plans
questions: What is one goal you want to achieve in the next few years?
How are you preparing for that goal?
What difficulty might you face?

selection_reason: Work/future Part 1 tasks support career vocabulary and short extended reasoning.

title: Social Interaction: Environment and Community

topics.name: Environment
questions: What environmental problem do you notice in your area?
Do you try to save energy or reduce waste? How?
Should schools teach more about environmental protection? Why?

topics.name: Community
questions: What makes a community a good place to live?
Have you ever joined a community activity?
How can young people help their local community?

selection_reason: Environment/community topics prepare learners for socially oriented opinions and examples.

title: Social Interaction: Culture and Entertainment

topics.name: Culture
questions: What festival is important in your country?
How do people usually celebrate it?
Do young people still care about traditional festivals? Why?

topics.name: Entertainment
questions: What do you do for entertainment?
Do you prefer watching films at home or at the cinema?
What kind of music or films do you dislike? Why?

selection_reason: Culture and entertainment are accessible Part 1 domains with descriptive and preference language.

title: Solution Discussion: School Health Program

content.situation: Your school wants to help students become healthier, but it can choose only one new program this term.
solutions: Add one more physical education class each week
Provide healthier food in the school cafeteria
Organize monthly health workshops
content.task: Choose the best solution. Explain why it is better than the other two options.

selection_reason: Part 2 requires choosing one of three solutions and comparing practical advantages.

title: Solution Discussion: Traffic near Campus

content.situation: Traffic around your university campus is crowded and unsafe during rush hour.
solutions: Create a free shuttle bus from nearby stations
Build more parking areas for motorbikes and cars
Limit private vehicles near the main gate at peak times
content.task: Choose the best solution and explain why it would solve the problem most effectively.

selection_reason: Traffic solution tasks are close to common VSTEP urban topics and require trade-off language.

title: Solution Discussion: Improving an English Club

content.situation: The English club at your college has fewer members than before. The organizers want to make it more useful.
solutions: Invite guest speakers every month
Hold weekly speaking games and debates
Create an online group for sharing learning materials
content.task: Choose one solution and explain why it would attract more students.

selection_reason: Education club scenarios train familiar solution vocabulary and reasons for learner engagement.

title: Solution Discussion: Reducing Plastic at a Café

content.situation: A popular campus café wants to reduce single-use plastic without losing customers.
solutions: Give discounts to customers who bring reusable cups
Replace plastic packaging with paper packaging
Charge a small fee for plastic cups and bags
content.task: Choose the most practical solution and discuss its advantages and disadvantages.

selection_reason: Environmental solution tasks encourage B2 comparison and feasibility reasoning.

title: Solution Discussion: Reducing Workplace Stress

content.situation: A company has noticed that many employees feel stressed and tired at work.
solutions: Offer flexible working hours
Provide free counselling sessions
Reduce the number of weekly meetings
content.task: Choose the best policy and explain why it would improve employee well-being.

selection_reason: Workplace well-being is a realistic B2 speaking situation with clear policy options.

title: Solution Discussion: Tourism Promotion Budget

content.situation: A small town wants to attract more visitors but has a limited tourism budget.
solutions: Improve the town's official travel website
Organize a weekend cultural festival
Train local people to become volunteer tour guides
content.task: Choose the option that would bring the greatest benefit and justify your choice.

selection_reason: Tourism solution tasks connect economy, culture and community benefits in Part 2 format.

title: Solution Discussion: Modernizing a Library

content.situation: Your local library wants to attract more young people.
solutions: Buy more digital books and online resources
Create quiet group-study rooms
Hold free weekend workshops for students
content.task: Choose one solution and explain why it would be most useful for young people.

selection_reason: Library modernization is an education/community task with accessible B1 language.

title: Solution Discussion: Neighborhood Safety

content.situation: People in your neighborhood are worried about safety at night.
solutions: Install better street lighting
Organize a neighborhood watch group
Ask the local authority for more police patrols
content.task: Choose the best solution and explain why it would make residents feel safer.

selection_reason: Community safety supports practical recommendation language and clear comparison.

title: Topic Development: Technology in Education

content.prompt: Discuss how technology has changed the way students learn. You may talk about access to materials, teacher-student interaction, independent learning and possible risks.
follow_up_questions: Do you think online learning can replace traditional classrooms?
What skills do students need to learn effectively online?
How can teachers prevent students from becoming distracted by technology?

selection_reason: Part 3 education-technology topic supports extended argument and follow-up discussion.

title: Topic Development: Public Transport and Cities

content.prompt: Discuss why public transport is important for city life. You may consider traffic, the environment, cost and convenience.
follow_up_questions: What makes people choose private vehicles instead of public transport?
Should public transport be free for students? Why?
How can cities make buses or trains more attractive?

selection_reason: Urban transport Part 3 tasks train development of reasons, examples and policy evaluation.

title: Topic Development: Responsible Social Media Use

content.prompt: Discuss the role of social media in modern life. You may talk about communication, news, business and personal well-being.
follow_up_questions: Do the benefits of social media outweigh the risks?
How has social media changed the way people get news?
What can parents do to help teenagers use social media responsibly?

selection_reason: Social media appears frequently in VSTEP-style speaking samples and supports balanced discussion.

title: Topic Development: Environmental Responsibility

content.prompt: Discuss who should take the greatest responsibility for protecting the environment: individuals, businesses or governments. You may use your own examples.
follow_up_questions: Can individual actions make a real difference?
Should companies pay higher taxes if they pollute?
What environmental policy would be most useful in your country?

selection_reason: C1 environment topics elicit abstract argument, concession and policy vocabulary.

title: Topic Development: Work-life Balance

content.prompt: Discuss why work-life balance is important. You may talk about health, family life, productivity and modern working styles.
follow_up_questions: Is remote work always good for work-life balance?
What should employers do to support their staff?
Would you accept a higher salary if it meant working much longer hours?

selection_reason: Work-life balance is a practical B2 topic linking health, work and family domains.

title: Topic Development: Tourism and Culture

content.prompt: Discuss how tourism affects local culture. You may talk about jobs, traditions, the environment and local people's daily lives.
follow_up_questions: Should governments limit the number of tourists in popular places?
How can tourists show respect for local culture?
What kind of tourism is most beneficial for a country?

selection_reason: Tourism Part 3 tasks support multi-angle argumentation and culturally relevant examples.

title: Topic Development: Healthy Lifestyle for Students

content.prompt: Discuss what students can do to stay healthy while studying. You may talk about sleep, food, exercise and stress management.
follow_up_questions: Why do some students have unhealthy habits?
Should schools provide more health education?
What healthy habit is easiest for you to maintain?

selection_reason: Health topics are accessible for B1 learners while supporting organized extended speaking.

title: Topic Development: AI and Future Jobs

content.prompt: Discuss how artificial intelligence may change employment in the future. You may consider productivity, job loss, new skills and ethical concerns.
follow_up_questions: Which jobs are least likely to be replaced by AI?
Should governments regulate AI in the workplace?
How should universities prepare students for an AI-driven economy?

selection_reason: AI and work is a C1-ready topic that encourages sophisticated vocabulary and nuanced opinions.
